extraction.one_line_summary: MARTIN Dan attacked early on the final climb into a headwind, holding off THOMAS Geraint and PORTE Richie who couldn't bridge despite fresh legs.
tactical_patterns: MARTIN Dan attacked when teammates exhausted on first part of climb, exploiting lack of organized chase structure despite headwind.
PORTE Richie worked for VAN AVERMAET's yellow jersey rather than GC ambitions, revealing BMC prioritized jersey defense over stage.
Quick-Step's early aggression drained rival teams, allowing BMC to conserve firepower for finale and protect VAN AVERMAET's yellow.
SKUJIŅŠ Toms positioned at front when breakaway caught, opportunistically collecting KOM points through tactical awareness not strength.

rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: won into headwind after long solo effort despite terrible season form, legs suddenly matched mental strength
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: couldn't bridge to Martin but showed exceptional power trying, signaling strong GC form ahead of mountains
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: neutral: rode for VAN AVERMAET's yellow instead of own ambitions, team strategy overriding personal GC opportunity
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: retained yellow through BMC tactics when appeared likely to lose it, cobbles stage looming suits him
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: extended green jersey record to 91 days but wasn't present in finale, prioritizing points over stage result
rider_signals.SKUJIŅŠ Toms: positive: second consecutive day collecting points through positioning intelligence, predicted easy KOM retention before stage
